question: A **ministerial regulation** (rank 4 on the pyramid) contradicts a **parliamentary statute** (rank 2). **Drag the highlight up the pyramid** to the rank of the norm that prevails — the losing norm shatters when you commit.
hints: Lower number = higher rank on this pyramid.
Lex superior: hierarchy beats everything else.
explanation: The statute (rank 2) wins — a regulation contradicting a law isn’t an alternative reading, it is **void**. Time and specialty never even enter the analysis when ranks differ.

question: Order these norms from highest to lowest rank in the Spanish system.
items: An international treaty published in Spain
An ordinary law passed by Parliament
A royal decree issued by the government
hints: Start from the norm every other norm must obey.
Custom only applies where all written law is silent.
explanation: Constitution → treaties → laws → regulations → custom: each level draws its validity from the one above and can be struck down for contradicting it.

question: Classify each norm by its position in the hierarchy.

rows.label: Government decree-law on urgent energy measures

explanation: Organic laws and decree-laws share the rank of law; ministerial orders are executive regulations; merchant usage is custom — last in line and only if proved.

question: Sort each conflict by the criterion that resolves it.

items.label: A regulation contradicts the statute it implements
Commercial sale rules vs general civil sale rules
Two successive ordinary laws on the same tax
Consumer-contract regime vs general contract law
A ministerial order conflicts with the Constitution
This year’s VAT reform vs last year’s

explanation: Different rank → hierarchy. Same rank, different scope → specialty. Same rank and scope → the later norm governs.

question: Match each source of law to its defining feature.

pairs.right: Urgent government norm, later ratified by Parliament
Executive rule that develops and details laws
Good faith and other values informing the whole system

explanation: Who makes the norm and under what conditions determines its rank — and its vulnerability to challenge.
